tax: Placed at order rank, Pyric Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a fuel-and-air-dependent combustion body, not a molten-rock one. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct combustion mode with its own fuel and ignition trigger.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a flame-ball or a creeping glow-front moving along fuel. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — scorch-rings and soot, or a creeping ash-band through mat — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Pyric Elementals occupies gas-seeps, oil stores, and combustible mat. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a fire-pressure spark ignites a fuel pocket or detritus bed and recur with the vent refills or the mat regrows and re-ignites, sustaining themselves on combustion of ambient fuel at high or low oxygen.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members chase fuel and air, spreading as flame or smoulder, dying the moment fuel or air fails. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a combustion body, immunity to fire, and instant failure on smothering, flooding, or fuel-loss. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: ignition, spread, flare, and dazzle. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Pyric Elementals matters because they turn fuel pockets and mat into spreading-fire traps that kill whole crews. It lets survey reports sort fire hazards by mode, so a crew knows whether to shutter lamps or rod-test the floor without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Pyric Elementals is the order-rank grouping of fire elementals whose bodies are open combustion — leaping flame, creeping smoulder, or sustained bipedal flame-form (Ignis-Kin) — within the True Elementals. Its members share a body that is the combustion itself, wholly dependent on fuel and air, and divide below into family groups by the mode of burning: open leaping flame (Flamelings), hidden creeping smoulder (Cinderlings), and vent-sustained humanoid form (Ignis-Kin). Pyric elementals are the fastest-spreading hazard in the worked tiers because their body is the fire.
